pages.purpose: Landing page introducing Demeo and its core AI GTM workflow.

important_elements.label: Open Dashboard
important_elements.why: Primary path into the app from the landing page.

important_elements.label: Get Started
important_elements.why: Starts founder setup for company context.

important_elements.label: Agent Control Panel
important_elements.why: Shows the multi-agent concept on the homepage.

important_elements.label: Prompt textarea
important_elements.selector_hint: textarea[placeholder="Write a launch email for our new analytics product targeting engineering teams…"]
important_elements.why: Visible example input for content generation.

pages.purpose: Founder setup page for entering company and product context.

important_elements.label: Company name input
important_elements.why: Required setup field for company identity.

important_elements.label: One-liner description
important_elements.selector_hint: textarea[placeholder="We help B2B SaaS teams turn cold outreach into qualified pipeline with AI demo rooms."]

important_elements.label: Target audience input
important_elements.selector_hint: input[placeholder="Solo founders, lean B2B sales teams, Series A startups"]
important_elements.why: Defines who the demo and content should speak to.

important_elements.label: Product/service description
important_elements.selector_hint: textarea[placeholder="Describe what your product does, its core value proposition, and how it works..."]
important_elements.why: Provides product context for downstream agents.

important_elements.label: Testing credentials textarea
important_elements.selector_hint:
textarea[placeholder="e.g.
Test API Key: sk-test-xxx
Sandbox URL: https://sandbox.example.com
Test User: demo@example.com / password123"]

allowed_actions.label: Highlight setup fields
allowed_actions.intent: Show what the founder provides during setup

allowed_actions.label: Go to Agent Lab
allowed_actions.intent: Move to the agent overview after setup context

pages.purpose: Agent lab showing the available specialist agents and demo room entry.
Command center showing onboarding status and quick actions.
Content generation workflow for posts, email, landing page, and blog post.

important_elements.label: Example prompt button
important_elements.selector_hint: button:has-text("Create social media posts announcing our product launch")

allowed_actions.label: Highlight prompt box
allowed_actions.intent: Show how content is generated from founder context

allowed_actions.label: Go to Setup
allowed_actions.intent: Prompt founder to add company context

pages.purpose: Legal guidance workflow with topic tabs and retry state.

important_elements.label: Try again button
important_elements.why: Non-destructive retry for the failed load.

allowed_actions.label: Highlight retry state
allowed_actions.intent: Show an example of a recoverable workflow state

pages.purpose: Research workflow for competitors, market sizing, audience, and trends.

allowed_actions.label: Highlight research prompt
allowed_actions.intent: Show how the user asks for competitor analysis

pages.purpose: Prospect-facing AI demo room where the buyer can ask questions.

important_elements.label: What's the ROI? prompt
important_elements.why: Safe starting question for the prospect.

important_elements.label: Ask anything input
important_elements.why: Main interaction point for the prospect.

important_elements.label: End & view CRM
important_elements.why: Moves to the founder outcome summary.

allowed_actions.label: Highlight demo room intro
allowed_actions.intent: Show the buyer asks questions in a personalized demo room

allowed_actions.label: End & view CRM
allowed_actions.intent: Show post-demo lead summary and follow-up

pages.purpose: CRM/lead summary page showing score, objections handled, and generated follow-up.

important_elements.label: Generated follow-up email
important_elements.why: Shows what the founder receives after the demo.

flows.goal: Show the full founder journey from setup to lead follow-up.

steps.say: First, the founder provides company and product context.
They can save that profile for downstream agents.
Next, open the demo room agent.
A prospect gets a personalized demo link and asks questions here.
When the demo ends, Demeo shows the founder the CRM summary and follow-up.
This is the qualified lead outcome and generated follow-up.

flows.goal: Show how AI output is generated from founder context.

steps.say: Open the agent lab from the dashboard.
The prompt box is where the founder asks for content.
If needed, return to setup to add better context.

approved_answers.topic: What does the founder need to provide?
approved_answers.answer: A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

approved_answers.topic: Should the runtime click the real app directly?
approved_answers.answer: The MVP should use an approved manifest and visual event playback. Playwright is better for setup-time extraction and validation. Live app control can come later for approved low-risk paths.
Yes. Gemini Live or a LiveKit voice agent can call the same safe demo tools used by text: show page, move cursor, highlight, propose click, answer from knowledge, and update lead memory.

notes: Selector hints come only from crawled controls when available.
The /crm route is discovered via internal link and treated as part of the safe walkthrough.
Some buttons appear to be UI tabs or presets; they are not added as click actions unless their effect is clearly safe.

decisions.question: What does the founder need to provide?
decisions.reply: They provide product and buyer context so the demo can be personalized.

events.say: Here’s the founder setup page.
The founder adds company name, one-liner, target audience, product description, website, sandbox credentials, and setup details.
A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

decisions.reason: This matches the setup flow and approved answer, and the onboarding page is the safest visual proof.

decisions.question: Show me the agent/content console.
decisions.reply: Here’s where the founder turns setup context into generated output.

events.say: Start from the dashboard command center.
Open the agent lab.
Switch into the content workflow.
This is the content console.
The prompt box is where AI output is generated from founder context.

decisions.reason: The manifest provides a safe path from dashboard to agents to content, which directly demonstrates generation.

decisions.question: Show the prospect demo room.
decisions.reply: This is the buyer-facing demo room with a personalized question flow.

events.say: Here’s the prospect demo room.
The buyer can ask questions like ROI or integrations in a guided demo experience.

decisions.reason: The demo page is the approved buyer-facing experience and can be shown safely without extra clicks.

decisions.question: Show me what the founder receives after the demo.
decisions.reply: The founder gets a lead summary, qualification signal, and generated follow-up.

events.say: Here’s the post-demo CRM view.
This shows the lead score and qualified outcome.
The founder receives the CRM summary and generated follow-up email after the prospect finishes the demo.

decisions.reason: The CRM page is the approved destination for the founder’s post-demo outcome.

question_results.question: What does the founder need to provide?

events.say: Here’s the founder setup page.
The founder adds company name, one-liner, target audience, product description, website, sandbox credentials, and setup details.
A product or staging URL, sandbox credentials when needed, target persona, demo goals, a short walkthrough, approved Q&A, CTA, and qualification questions.

question_results.reason: This matches the setup flow and approved answer, and the onboarding page is the safest visual proof.

question_results.question: Show me the agent/content console.

events.say: Start from the dashboard command center.
Open the agent lab.
Switch into the content workflow.
This is the content console.
The prompt box is where AI output is generated from founder context.

question_results.reason: The manifest provides a safe path from dashboard to agents to content, which directly demonstrates generation.

question_results.question: Show the prospect demo room.

events.say: Here’s the prospect demo room.
The buyer can ask questions like ROI or integrations in a guided demo experience.

question_results.reason: The demo page is the approved buyer-facing experience and can be shown safely without extra clicks.

question_results.question: Show me what the founder receives after the demo.

events.say: Here’s the post-demo CRM view.
This shows the lead score and qualified outcome.
The founder receives the CRM summary and generated follow-up email after the prospect finishes the demo.

question_results.reason: The CRM page is the approved destination for the founder’s post-demo outcome.

success.success_rule: MVP-usable means at least 3/4 expected app areas discovered, at least 6 safe/reviewable actions, no invented manifest pages/action refs, and at least 3/4 prospect questions planned to the right page/action.
